comments.author: @bethgardner360
comments.text: When I hike down there if I saw both tourists, hikers and natives throwing trash. The tribal people riding horses up to the top of the trail, head and carrying peoples luggage down drink Gatorade, and throw the empty bottles on the trail. Hikers left trash behind at the campsite.  The horses appeared very parched, and when I asked one of the persons riding the horse, it was clearly a source of conflict. The people visiting are disrespectful and leaving the trash behind, but I also saw trash in backyards and all over. I hope there was more to see beyond the accessible area where the land was respected. It is challenging to remove trash from there unless it’s written out it has to be airlifted out. However, I was still quite surprised to see the lack of ownership to keep their outdoor spaces free from trash.  By the way, some of this video is questionable. The water was definitely beautiful and blue green, but some of these colors are not real.

comments.author: @eladnudd1895
comments.text: 😮 to be fair about this documentary.. there is a helicopter pad, with a fuel and maintenance station. And a radio tower that has two way communication thats also constantly maintained and  Satellites enabled, internet. Along with generators, small power plant. There is a water filtering station with pumps thats is constantly maintained. A clinic, the red cross visits. The people receive all shots, and dental appointments. Volunteer constantly visit, they receive quality food. They dont live in tee pee's or huts,, yes they are isolated but not uncivilized. And the proceeds from gov, " they receive money" and the money from tourist goes into many things they have plumbing, ac, and refrigerators. They don't wash clothes nor themselves in the river.. piping and sewers. This documentary gives the impression they live as there ancestors did...or somewhere close to that. They dress like you and me except for the tourist, they have a nice community building, to play bingo, movies, parties...

comments.author: @evelynwilson1566
comments.text:
A very beautiful place. In glad the tribe have returned. What a job, taking the mule train down, although coming back up would be harder😀. Why is it surprising that the mules have the trail memorised?. I would think the people do too. The mules can probably find their way in the dark too.

I live in Scotland and many children in remote areas here stay in school hostels during the week and go home at weekends.

It is difficult balance between letting people in, so they love the place
 and want to care for it and managing that so they don't do too much damage. Not an easy job for the tribe.

I hope the Uranium mining doesn't get approved. I pray for the people there, I hope they can continue to live in and safeguard their sacred place.

comments.author: @carlbittner9691
comments.text: I've made 4 backpacking trips to Havasupai, last one in 1996. (I live in Arizona) Besides the natural beauty of the final half mile along the creek into the village, there is an unique scent from the vegetation that grows alongside of the creek. It's as unique as the scent is from a creasote bush after a rain, but definitely different, more sweeter. If you planning to go, reserve at least 3 nights, 4 is better. I've been to many places around the world, Havasupai is the closest to paradise I've been to. I would not advise going in June, July, or August if you're backpacking. It gets over 100 during the day and it's all uphill going out.

replies.author: @bethgardner360
replies.text: Those are off so prime months for monsoon rains where it is known to flood

replies.text: Been there a few times as well. I lived in Az for 9 years. I take my time on the  8 mile hike down but I take a mule up. That works for me ~ it’s been since 1993 since I was there though … not sure if this is still an option

comments.author: @davidsmartt9007
comments.text:
Let me see if I get this right, they have their own reservation, they are their own Nation inside a Nation and can tell the rest of America whether or not they can access their reservation.  Do they pay local/state/federal/taxes? 

Are they free to travel outside the reservation w/o having to pay fees?  Do they get any government assistance like food stamps/Medicare/Medicaid/SSA payments?

If they dont have to pay us a steep fee to access our Nation's land/roads/cities then why do we have to pay this tribe thousands of dollars just to visit and hike on their land for whole whopping 3 days.  

 Why do we have to pay for them to have an American Post Office on their own National land?   Since they are independent & have their own Nation then they can invent, finance and run a Reservation postal system on their own land.

They lost the Indian Wars and were conquered by the new America, yet unlike most of the rest of the world we still gave them land (granted not always the best land) so they could continue to live their primitive lifestyle despite having been conquered by us and despite all indicators and signs pointing to the fact that this was totally out of step and in conflict with the scientifically advancing Western Civilization that now occupied the America where they had lived.  

Every modern nation today has borders that outlines its physical geography as a nation because at one time or an other some group of people went exploring and found a new land, they liked what they saw, they liked the types or amounts of natural resources available in this new land so they did what humans have been doing to other humans ever since we drove the Neanderthals into extinction.   

The invading culture decided that this land, which was new to them but occupied by others, was worth fighting & dying for so they attcked, fought a war and then won the war.  
In 98% of cases where one group conquers another in order to take and try to keep the other group's land and resources...............
(heck, those people themselves probably had taken this same land from an even older group of peoples who had occupied the land before they themselves had arrived),.............. the peoples who lost and surrendered and then would have  either had to escape to a new land themselves, or the survivors would have been killed off or sold off as slaves, or they just would have been absorbed by the new country and culture so that after several generations the original blood line would be so diluted that it would be hard to tell them apart from the rest of the new Nation. 

So unless they pay thousands of dollars for a whopping 3 day stay, (which means that only the wealthy will get to see it), I understand controlling the access to a fragile ecosystem but they should do this by limiting the # of permits issued per day, not by charging such a steep price per 3 day permit so that only the rich can afford.

It's 2026, none of these so called Indians still truely live like their ancestors, at any time in the past any other Indian tribe in the area could have raided them and then as is on par for all of known Indian history, that the conquering Indian Tribe would have either tortured and killed whoever survived the attack  or they would have kidnapped and enslaved the survivors.

The American Indian was not some peace loving tree hugger.  Nope, they were cruel to anyone they caught who was outside their tribe.  They would attack and murder or enslave their neighbors just to steal their resources, what we did to them was nothing different than what Indians had been doing to Indians for thousands of yrs. 

They were inherently violent because they lived in a harsh and violent world, but that world and all of its violence existed long before any Europeans or Whites  initially arrived and it still existed later when America became a Nation itself.

Im just really  tired  of people trying to push Indians off as some kind of peace loving, tree hugging, gentle nature lovers fully in tune with their environment and mother nature.

 They were stone aged pathalogical brutes who would use violence as their primary means of communication outside of their tribe, whether it be another Indian tribe or white settlers.

They 100% are not this now and never have been in the past.  They were just as violent, cruel and homicidal as their white counter parts were.  
However, since they had not progressed past their original primitive stone age culture [at least not until after they had contact w/ whites where they obtained horses and metal to make better weapons with which, in turn, just made their homicidal and violent nature that much more efficient], their ability to wage war prior to white contact was limited both because they only had the use of primitive stone age type weapons like war clubs, obsidian or flint knife/arrow/spear heads and because they were also fairly restricted geographically because the only mode of transportation that they had invented was their feet, they had no concept of the wheel, their only ability to carry stuff when traveling was to use domesticated dogs to drag a sled made of 2 poles tied together and hitched to the dog, then with an animal hide hammock lashed between the poles to hold what ever they needed transported.  

So invading and raiding your neighbors by foot and in small parties, with hand made weapons probably took them a lot more time and effort to do since they would have had to walk everywhere (at least until they were able to capture and use some of the Spanish ponies that had been left to run wild.  

This may have improved the distance and frequency of raids initially but once they got the horse from the Spanish they became much more efficient at raiding/enslaving/murdering any other Indian tribe that they could reach and beat.

Modern Indians are nothing like the native Indians from the 1800s, they are no longer stone age hunter gathers w/ some limited cultivation/farming capabilities.  They are not even a shadow of themselves, most likely because they have grown dependent on the government and tribe and  much less dependent upon themselves.   Even w/ all the land that has been given back to them they have failed to use those resources to advance their own people

Most of the Indian wars in the west were over by the 1890's and I can understand giving them their own land but there should have been some kind of time constraints (like a 250 yr rent free lease, or have some kind of  ancestral constraints, such as you must be at least 50% native American if you are to recieve tribal benefits, once the tribe as a whole is diluted genetically to less than 50%  using modern DNA testing then they should not be part of any reservation, the reservation should revert back to Federal ownership.  

The modern Indian is absolutely nothing like their old ancestors.  Just like so many of todays modern African American Men, many of the Indian Men have lost their way because they have become dependent upon the bottle and what ever handouts that the tribe or the State/Federal governments want to hand out to them.

So many modern "Indians" are now drunks and cowards who spend more time drinking, doing drugs, beating and killing their own women  and abusing their own children (or at a bare minimum failing to provide for, teach and raise up their children properly so that they can break the modern cycle of  dependancy/domestic abuse/child neglect/ETOH abuse, drug use and gang activity.

I would think the most successful modern Indians are the young ones who are smart enough to leave the "poverty & drug trap" of the reservation and join the rest of the American society by going to college to get a degree/license or by learning a trade that they can then find work in so that they can become independent and able to join the larger American society in whole by getting and staying off of that "pretty death trap" that their reservations have become.

If they want their own Nation, if they can tell the rest of America when and where we can go on those reservations then its only fair that they not enjoy all the perks of the American society.

They should have to stay on their reservation and if they want to leave their Nation  to partake in any "non reservation" activities such as to work/go shopping/accessing public services or to access other public places in our 'Nation' then they should have to pay a fee to the local/state/federal governments to access our Nation's land/activities/resources.

 Hell I probably have as much Indian in me as a lot of so called "Native Americans", (can you say Elizabeth Warren/Pocahontas).  I really think we should be tracking the genetic profile of anyone allowed to live on a reservation.  As soon as they have more '(>50%) non Native American Indian' blood than they do Native American Indian blood then they should no longer be considered an Indian since they now have more 'American'  genetics than they have Native American genetics, so why give them land that they didn't pay for and where they no longer (by % of genome) are genetically a Native American.

Few other countries would let their conquered people's become a separate Nation inside a Nation.  They would have killed the survivors or kidnapped and sold/traded them off as slaves, but few countries would give them land and let them tell the winning side what they can and cant do.

Humans gave always killed other humans over land and resources and its not going to change any time soon as we are a selfish and violent species.
